question_text: A balloon is filled with helium and sealed at sea level where atmospheric pressure is normal. If you take the balloon to the top of a mountain where atmospheric pressure is much lower, what will happen to the balloon and why?

options.label: A
options.text: It shrinks because the helium gets colder at higher altitude

options.label: B
options.text: It expands because the pressure inside the balloon is now greater than the atmospheric pressure outside

options.label: C
options.text: It stays the same size because helium is lighter than air

options.label: D
options.text: It shrinks because the number of helium particles decreases

correct_answer: It expands because the pressure inside the balloon is now greater than the atmospheric pressure outside
explanation: When the sealed balloon is taken to the mountain, the helium gas inside maintains its pressure (it's sealed). However, the atmospheric pressure outside decreases significantly at higher altitude. This creates a pressure difference: the constant internal pressure now pushes outward harder than the weaker external pressure pushes inward, causing the balloon to expand.
